top_signals.title: Taiwan Lifts 2026 Growth Outlook to More Than 9% on AI Hunger
top_signals.summary: Taiwan revised up its economic growth forecast for this year as demand for tech goods in the AI era tops worries about the Iran war boosting energy costs.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Iran reportedly launches missiles as Trump mulls deal to pause war for two months
top_signals.summary: The latest military and economic actions come as President Trump insists he feels no pressure to make a deal with Iran before November's midterm elections.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: ECB’s Panetta States Case for Rate Hike Without Pre-Set Path
top_signals.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Fabio Panetta acknowledged the case for an interest-rate hike while urging colleagues not to flag subsequent tightening.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Ukraine to buy 20 new Gripen jets, Sweden to donate older jets sooner
top_signals.summary: “We need these jets, and for us this is really a new page for Ukraine,” Zelenskyy said.

Labor shortage fuels ramp-up of humanoid robot development

top_signals.title: Most planned natural gas pipeline capacity additions in 2026 and 2027 originate in Texas
top_signals.summary: Developers plan to bring approximately 44.9 billion cubic feet per day (Bcf/d) of new pipeline capacity online in the United States in 2026 and 2027, according to our latest Natural Gas Pipeline Projects Tracker. Approximately 70% (31.6 Bcf/d) of this new capacity is already under construction. More than 66% (29.7 Bcf/d) of the capacity additions originate in Texas. Louisiana is second with 19% (8.4 Bcf/d) of total capacity additions.
